tossups.question: Kirkus Reviews describes this work as, “written with a fervor that is both fierce and compassionate,” and this work asks, “Eagle without wings. Here we bide our time. And what are we going to do about it?” One character in this work describes the “endless fatal leap toward tomorrow, and how the city this novel takes place in as, “there is only outrage, never tragedy.” Two characters in this work are the writer Rodrigo Pola, and a woman trying to improve her social status, Norma Larragoiti. Another prominent character in this novel is the enigmatic Ixca Cienfuegos. This work was published in 1958 as its author’s first novel, and is based on a wealthy businessman who turns his back on his non- Mexican ethnicity, Federico Robles. For ten points, name this Carlos Fuentes work based in Mexico City about a place where one can presumably breathe easier.
tossups.question_sanitized: Kirkus Reviews describes this work as, "written with a fervor that is both fierce and compassionate," and this work asks, "Eagle without wings. Here we bide our time. And what are we going to do about it?" One character in this work describes the "endless fatal leap toward tomorrow, and how the city this novel takes place in as, "there is only outrage, never tragedy." Two characters in this work are the writer Rodrigo Pola, and a woman trying to improve her social status, Norma Larragoiti. Another prominent character in this novel is the enigmatic Ixca Cienfuegos. This work was published in 1958 as its author's first novel, and is based on a wealthy businessman who turns his back on his non- Mexican ethnicity, Federico Robles. For ten points, name this Carlos Fuentes work based in Mexico City about a place where one can presumably breathe easier.
tossups.answer_sanitized: Where the Air is Clear or [la region mas transparente]

bonuses.leadin: Many Latin American literary works have been inspired by revolutions. For 10 points each:
parts: In this 1958 novel, the former revolutionary Federico Robles (“ROH-blez”) marries Norma Laragoiti in the years following the Mexican Revolution.
This author, who included Men of Maize in his Banana Trilogy , was inspired by the 1954 overthrow of President Jacobo Árbenz to write the collection Weekend in Guatemala .
An essay on “The Spanish Republic and” this country’s “Revolution” was written by José Martí, whose poems were adapted into this country’s national song “Guantanamera.”
answers: Where the Air Is Clear [or La región más transparente ] (by Carlos Fuentes)
Cuba [or Republic of Cuba or República de Cuba ; accept “The Spanish Republic and the Cuba n Revolution”]
answers_sanitized: Where the Air Is Clear [or  La region mas transparente ] (by Carlos Fuentes)
Cuba [or Republic of  Cuba or Republica de  Cuba ; accept "The Spanish Republic and the  Cuba n Revolution"]
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Many Latin American literary works have been inspired by revolutions. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: In this 1958 novel, the former revolutionary Federico Robles ("ROH-blez") marries Norma Laragoiti in the years following the Mexican Revolution.
This author, who included Men of Maize in his Banana Trilogy , was inspired by the 1954 overthrow of President Jacobo Arbenz to write the collection Weekend in Guatemala .
An essay on "The Spanish Republic and" this country's "Revolution" was written by Jose Marti, whose poems were adapted into this country's national song "Guantanamera."

bonuses.leadin: This character titles a novel whose whimsically named-characters include a prostitute named Dorian Dolor, and Dante Loredano, who has imaginary conversations with this character. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this character, who in Carlos Fuentes’ last novel, is reincarnated once every year for 24 hours. In that novel, this man appears at the Hotel Metropol and explains eternal recurrence to Fuentes himself.
Nietzsche on His Balcony was first published in 2016 by this publisher, which also brought out Fuentes’s The Great Latin American Novel. It is named for a book by Flann O’Brien.
Dalkey Archive Press also published this Fuentes novel, which takes its name from a line from Vision de Anahuac and centers on Ixca Cienfuegos in a post-revolutionary Mexico City.
answers_sanitized: Friedrich Nietzsche [or Nietzsche on his Balcony]
Where the Air is Clear [or La region mas transparente]
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: This character titles a novel whose whimsically named-characters include a prostitute named Dorian Dolor, and Dante Loredano, who has imaginary conversations with this character. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this character, who in Carlos Fuentes' last novel, is reincarnated once every year for 24 hours. In that novel, this man appears at the Hotel Metropol and explains eternal recurrence to Fuentes himself.
Nietzsche on His Balcony was first published in 2016 by this publisher, which also brought out Fuentes's The Great Latin American Novel. It is named for a book by Flann O'Brien.
Dalkey Archive Press also published this Fuentes novel, which takes its name from a line from Vision de Anahuac and centers on Ixca Cienfuegos in a post-revolutionary Mexico City.

bonuses.leadin: A character in this novel recites a line from a poem by Gerard Nerval about the importance of the present before entering a cantina and being shot to death. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this novel that opens with Ixca Cienfuegos declaring “in Mexico City there is never tragedy but only outrage.” It follows the lives of Mexico City residents like business tycoon Federico Robles.

Another business tycoon created by the author of Where the Air is Clear is this man who takes over Don Gamaliel Bernal’s estate and marries his daughter Catalina in a novel that he narrates from his deathbed.
This Mexican author, one of the major writers of the Latin American Boom, wrote Where the Air is Clear and The Death of Artemio Cruz.
answers_sanitized: Where the Air is Clear [or La region mas transparente]
